description: FilmCrop Standalone Engine - Extract core detection algorithm into a Python package, build a PyQt6 GUI for direct TIFF/DNG processing, and make Lightroom plugin an optional result importer via XMP/HTTP/JSON

userStories.title: Modularize core algorithm into filmcrop Python package
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to extract the detection engine from detect_thumb.py into a proper Python package so that the standalone GUI and future tools can import it directly.
acceptanceCriteria: Create filmcrop/ directory with __init__.py
Extract analyze_thumb, build_frames, detect_long_edges, _detect_scan_edge, _load_image_array into filmcrop/detector.py
Create filmcrop/export.py with to_json(frames), to_xmp(frames), crop_and_save(image, frames, output_dir) stubs
Refactor detect_thumb.py CLI to import from filmcrop.detector instead of inline code
python -m filmcrop.detector --help works or existing tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Completed in first iteration. Package already existed; fixed mypy type errors and added CLI entry point to detector.py.

userStories.title: PyQt6 main window and image display
userStories.description: As a user, I want to open a TIFF/DNG and see it rendered in a modern GUI so that I can work with high-resolution scans smoothly.
acceptanceCriteria: Create filmcrop/gui/ directory with main_window.py
Implement QMainWindow with QGraphicsView + QGraphicsScene
Add File > Open menu supporting .tif .tiff .jpg .jpeg .png .dng
TIFF/JPEG/PNG loaded via PIL with _load_image_array logic (16-bit support)
DNG loaded via rawpy fallback or placeholder message if rawpy unavailable
Status bar shows image dimensions, bit depth, load time
Wheel zoom from 10% to 400% works smoothly
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: GUI already existed; fixed mypy type errors, added bit depth to status bar, and created __main__.py entry point.

userStories.title: One-click frame detection in GUI
userStories.description: As a user, I want to click a button and see automatic frame boundary detection so that I don't have to manually draw every frame.
acceptanceCriteria: Add Detect Frames toolbar button and menu item
Dialog for expected frame count (default 6, auto=0)
Call filmcrop.detector.analyze_image() with current image path
Display detected frames as colored rectangles overlaid on QGraphicsScene
Show detection time and memory in status bar
Error dialog if detection fails with traceback
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Added Tools > Detect Frames menu item, memory usage display in status bar (via optional psutil), and QMessageBox error dialog with full traceback on detection failure.

userStories.title: Drag-to-adjust boundaries and frame management
userStories.description: As a user, I want to drag frame boundaries with the mouse and add/remove frames so that auto-detection errors are easy to fix.
acceptanceCriteria: Mouse hover on frame edge shows resize cursor
Drag boundary line updates pixel coordinates in real time
Right panel lists all frames with pixel and relative coordinates
Selected frame highlighted, others semi-transparent
Add Frame / Delete Frame buttons update list and canvas
Undo (Ctrl+Z) and Redo (Ctrl+Shift+Z) with at least 20-step history
Spinbox precise editing syncs with canvas display
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Fixed label position sync during canvas drag and spinbox editing. Frame list now displays both pixel and relative coordinates. Added public frame_data() accessor to DraggableFrameItem.

userStories.title: Export JSON sidecar
userStories.description: As a user, I want to save frame boundaries to a JSON file so that Lightroom or other tools can read them.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement to_json() in filmcrop/export.py
File > Export > JSON Sidecar menu item
Default path <image_name>.filmcrop.json in same directory
JSON format 100% compatible with detect_thumb.py output
Each frame has pixel coords (top/bottom/left/right) and relative coords
Status bar confirms export path
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Added optional debug parameter to to_json() for 100% detect_thumb.py compatibility. Added File > Export submenu with JSON and XMP sidecar items. MainWindow now stores debug info after detection and passes it to to_json().

userStories.title: Export XMP sidecar (Adobe standard)
userStories.description: As a user, I want to save frame boundaries as XMP sidecars so that Lightroom can natively read crop rectangles.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement to_xmp() in filmcrop/export.py using python-xmp-toolkit or hand-written XML
File > Export > XMP Sidecar menu item
Generate .xmp file per frame or single file with custom filmcrop namespace
Include xmpMM:DocumentID and dc:title fields
XMP structure validated against Adobe spec (basic check)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Rewrote to_xmp() with proper XML escaping, standard crs:Crop* fields for the first frame, xmp:CreateDate, and a validate_xmp() helper. MainWindow now stores crop_angle from detection results and passes it to to_xmp().

userStories.title: High-quality cropped image export
userStories.description: As a user, I want to export each frame as a standalone image so that I can use them outside of Lightroom.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement crop_and_save() in filmcrop/export.py using PIL Image.crop() + LANCZOS
File > Export > Cropped Images menu item
Options dialog: format (TIFF/JPEG/PNG), quality, color space
Default output dir <image_dir>/cropped/
Naming: <image_name>_frame_01.tif etc
16-bit TIFF preserved when input is 16-bit
Progress bar with cancel support
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Enhanced crop_and_save() to preserve 16-bit TIFF depth and ICC profiles. Added ExportDialog with format/quality/color-space options. Added File > Export > Cropped Images menu item. Default output dir is <image_dir>/cropped/. Verified with synthetic 16-bit TIFF test.

userStories.title: Local HTTP API server
userStories.description: As a Lightroom user, I want the standalone engine to expose a local HTTP API so that the Lightroom plugin can query it directly.
acceptanceCriteria: Create filmcrop/api.py with FastAPI app
POST /analyze endpoint: accepts image path + frame count, returns JSON detection result
POST /crop endpoint: accepts image path + frame coords, returns cropped file paths
GET /health endpoint returns service status
Tools > Start/Stop API Server menu items in GUI
Server runs in background thread, default port 8765
CORS restricted to localhost only
Status bar shows API address and request count
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Added request counting to all API endpoints, implemented proper uvicorn.Server shutdown via should_exit, added has_api() guard in GUI with QMessageBox error dialog, and added QTimer polling to display live request count in status bar.

userStories.title: Lightroom plugin XMP import mode
userStories.description: As a Lightroom user, I want the plugin to read XMP sidecars from the standalone engine so that I can import crops without the engine running.
acceptanceCriteria: Add Import FilmCrop XMP menu item to DetectFrames.lua
Directory picker scans for .filmcrop.xmp files
Parse XMP frame coordinates into Lua table
Create virtual copy per frame with applyDevelopSettings crop
Prompt overwrite or skip if virtual copy already exists
Batch import entire directory
Lightroom plugin loads without errors
userStories.notes: Implemented directory picker with .filmcrop.xmp scanning via Python fallback, filename-based XMP-to-photo matching, pre-flight duplicate detection with overwrite/skip dialog, and backward-compatible createVirtualCopiesFromFrames extension.

userStories.title: Lightroom plugin HTTP API mode
userStories.description: As a Lightroom user, I want the plugin to call the standalone engine via HTTP so detection runs externally and results appear in Lightroom automatically.
acceptanceCriteria: Add Detect via FilmCrop Engine menu item to DetectFrames.lua
Dialog for API address (default http://localhost:8765)
POST image path to /analyze using LrHttp or LuaSocket
Parse JSON response and create virtual copies with crops
Show progress bar for transmission and virtual copy creation
Error message if engine unreachable with startup instructions
Lightroom plugin loads without errors
userStories.notes: Enhanced detectViaHttp() with LrProgressScope for per-photo progress, Develop module guard, ProcessAgent.directionAlign for EXIF orientation correctness, expected frames dialog field synced to prefs, and improved HTTP error reporting via pcall second return value.

userStories.title: Lightroom plugin shared JSON watch mode
userStories.description: As a Lightroom user, I want the plugin to watch a shared JSON file so that edits in the standalone GUI are picked up by Lightroom.
acceptanceCriteria: Add Watch FilmCrop JSON menu item to DetectFrames.lua
File picker selects .filmcrop.json file
Poll file mtime every 2 seconds in background task
On change: auto-create/update virtual copies or prompt confirm
Stop Watching menu item terminates polling
Initial load applies current JSON immediately
Lightroom plugin loads without errors
